= Portuguese Opening, Miguel Gambit =
{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|=
|Portuguese Opening:Miguel Gambit|
  |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd| |nd|rd|=
  |pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|=

  | |bl|bd| |pd| | | |=

In this line, White attempts to directly challenge Black's control of the center with the sacrifice of the pawn on b4. As in the [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...Nc6/3. Bc4/3...Bc5/4. b4|Evans Gambit]], after Black's most obvious move 3...Bxb4, White will play 4. c3 with tempo, a move that will eventually allow him to play d4 and get two pawns in the center.
title: Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Bb5/2...Bc5/3. b4
